hook.text: Reply to Alice O'Neil's comment I had my surgery with a general gynaecologist, was in surgery for 30 minutes, told there was no endo and pain is in my head. No photos either to take to a specialist.
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Empathy and validation of patient frustration
hook.target_audience_raw: Women with endometriosis who have had or considering surgery with general gynecologists

main_claim.text: Patients undergoing laparoscopic surgery for suspected endometriosis should receive photos and a detailed analysis of findings, even if no endometriosis is found, to ensure understanding and proper follow-up.

supporting_explanation.summary: The doctor argues that photos allow patients to relate to findings, that absence of visible endometriosis does not mean pain is not real, and that a 30-minute procedure without photos is insufficient. He advises seeking a second opinion if such a situation occurs.

funnel_rationale: The video addresses a specific patient's surgical experience, educates on the importance of photos and detailed findings during diagnostic laparoscopy, and encourages seeking second opinions, which is typical of middle-of-funnel content for patients evaluating treatment options.

cta.wording: So I would ask for a second opinion if that was the case.
cta.requested_action_raw: Seek a second opinion from a specialist

secondary_raw: Patient rights, second opinions, surgical documentation
